name: Rice Stuffed Artichokes ( MW )
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut off the stem and loose outer leaves from the artichoke. Cut off 1 inch from the top. Snip off sharp leaf tips.
2. Brush the cut edges with lemon juice.
3. Place the artichoke and water in a small casserole dish. Cover with vented clear plastic wrap.
4. Cook on high power for 5 to 7 minutes or until tender, rotating the casserole halfway after 3 minutes.
5. Let stand, covered, while preparing the stuffing.
6. For the stuffing, stir together shredded carrot, sliced green onion, butter or margarine, and crushed sage in a small non-metal bowl.
7. Cook on high power for 1 to 2 1/2 minutes or until vegetables are tender, stirring once.
8. Stir together the vegetable mixture and cooked rice.
9. Drain the artichoke. Remove the center leaves and choke from the artichoke.
10. Spread the artichoke leaves slightly. Spoon the rice stuffing into the center and behind each large leaf.
11. Return the artichoke to the casserole. Cover with vented clear plastic wrap.
12. Cook on high power for 1 to 2 minutes or until the stuffing is hot and the base of the artichoke is fork-tender, rotating the casserole halfway every 30 seconds.
13. Let stand, covered, while preparing the sauce.
14. For the sauce, stir together chicken broth, lemon juice, cornstarch, and pepper in a 1-cup measure.
15. Cook uncovered on high power for 1 to 2 minutes or until thickened and bubbly, stirring every 30 seconds.
16. Stir half of the hot sauce into the egg yolk.
17. Return all to the 1-cup measure and cook uncovered for 15 seconds more.
18. Transfer the stuffed artichoke to a warm plate. Pour the sauce around the artichoke.
